name: Menus.Active
comment:
Little helper to define whether focus moving to an option on input change should
also select it.


# Definition

@docs Active

unions.name: Active
unions.comment:
This will focus the first matching option but not select it when input is changed

    { config...
    , visibleOptions =
        \str selected opts ->
            case List.head (List.filter ((==) str) opts) of
                Just match ->
                    Just ( Menus.Active.Focussed match, opts )

                Nothing ->
                    Nothing
    }

This will focus and select the first matching option when input is changed

    { config...
    , visibleOptions =
        \str selected opts ->
            case List.head (List.filter ((==) str) opts) of
                Just match ->
                    Just ( Menus.Active.FocussedAndSelected opt, opts )

                Nothing ->
                    Nothing
    }

name: Menus.Select
comment:
Define whether a selection should change or not

@docs Change

name: Preset.Combobox

unions.name: Config
unions.comment:
Created in [#config](#config)  
    This is passed into [#update](#update) and [#view](#view) and is used to control how the menu reacts to user input

unions.name: Context
unions.comment: Context is passed around internally, and is just used to track if the menu has been recently opened

unions.name: Model
unions.comment:
Stores whether the menu is open or not, and which option is currently focussed.  
    It does _not_ store which option is selected.  
    Is updated and returned in the [#update](#update) function.

unions.name: Token
unions.comment: Created automatically when you call [#view](#view), pass it in to view functions

aliases.name: CreatableConfig
aliases.comment:
Passed into [#creatable](#creatable)
    Define how to create a new option, given a string

aliases.name: OptionConfig
aliases.comment:
Passed in to [#config](#config)  
    - `id`: Must be unique, and is displayed in the HTML, used for accessibility  
    - `optionToLabel`: Allows users to use keyboard input to search for options  
    - `optionToId`: Uniquely identify each option
    - `optionToSelection`: Turn any option into your selection, for instance you might want the selection to be `Maybe option`,
        so you would have `optionToSelection = Just` 
    - `selectionToOption`: Given the current selection and a list of options, find the option that corresponds to the selection.
    - `selectionToLabel`: What to set as the menu input's value
    - `selectionCleared`: When the user presses backspace, while the menu is closed or the input is empty, what should happen to the selection?
        Returning `NotChanged` will make the combobox non-clearable.
    - `matchesInput`: Given a string and an option, does the option exactly match the string?
    - `containsInput`: Given a string and an option, does the option "contain" the string?
    - `visibleOptions`: The Preset does some work under the scenes to already filter down visible options using data passed in from this config object,
but there are a couple of "plugins", [#autoSelect](#autoSelect) and [#creatable](#creatable) you can use here to change how the combobox functions.
aliases.type: { id : String.String, optionToLabel : option -> String.String, optionToId : option -> String.String, optionToSelection : option -> selection, selectionToOption : selection -> List.List option -> Maybe.Maybe option, selectionToLabel : selection -> String.String, selectionCleared : Menus.Select.Change selection, matchesInput : String.String -> option -> Basics.Bool, containsInput : String.String -> option -> Basics.Bool, visibleOptions : Preset.Combobox.VisibleOptionConfig option selection -> String.String -> selection -> Maybe.Maybe ( Menus.Active.Active option, List.List option ) -> Maybe.Maybe ( Menus.Active.Active option, List.List option ) }

aliases.name: VisibleOptionConfig
aliases.comment: This is passed down to the [#autoSelect](#autoSelect) and [#creatable](#creatable) plugins from the main config
aliases.type: { optionToLabel : option -> String.String, optionToId : option -> String.String, optionToSelection : option -> selection, selectionToOption : selection -> List.List option -> Maybe.Maybe option, selectionToLabel : selection -> String.String, matchesInput : String.String -> option -> Basics.Bool, containsInput : String.String -> option -> Basics.Bool }

values.name: autoSelect
values.comment:
This plugin will automatically select the closest match to the input.
    If you are combining this with other plugins, use it last or any changes within the latter plugins won't be autoselected.


    Preset.Combobox.config
        { ...
        , visibleOptions =
            Preset.Combobox.autoSelect
        }
values.type: Preset.Combobox.VisibleOptionConfig option selection -> String.String -> selection -> Maybe.Maybe ( Menus.Active.Active option, List.List option ) -> Maybe.Maybe ( Menus.Active.Active option, List.List option )

values.name: config
values.comment:
Create a [#Config](#Config) type. You don't need to explicitly pass in the `Context` yourself,
    the [#update](#update) and [#view](#view) functions expect `Context -> Config option selection`.
values.type: Preset.Combobox.OptionConfig option selection -> Preset.Combobox.Context -> Preset.Combobox.Config option selection

values.name: creatable
values.comment:
This plugin allows people to create new options when they enter input that doesn't exactly match any of the options.


    Preset.Combobox.config
        { ...
        , visibleOptions =
            Preset.Combobox.creatable
                { newOption = \label -> NewOption label }
        }
values.type: Preset.Combobox.CreatableConfig option -> Preset.Combobox.VisibleOptionConfig option selection -> String.String -> selection -> Maybe.Maybe ( Menus.Active.Active option, List.List option ) -> Maybe.Maybe ( Menus.Active.Active option, List.List option )

values.name: focussedOption
values.comment: Returns the currently focussed option
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Token option selection -> Maybe.Maybe option

values.name: init
values.comment: Initialise a closed menu, you can use this in your own init function
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Model option

values.name: input
values.comment: The input used to open and close the menu
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Token option selection -> { placeholder : String.String } -> List.List (Html.Attribute (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)) -> Html.Html (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)

values.name: isOpen
values.comment: Check whether the menu is open or closed
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Token option selection -> Basics.Bool

values.name: menuClosed
values.comment: A [#Msg](#Msg) that is sent when the menu is closed
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Msg option

values.name: menuOpened
values.comment: A [#Msg](#Msg) that is sent when the menu is opened
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Msg option

values.name: option
values.comment: A focus and selectable option
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Token option selection -> { value : option, isSelected : Basics.Bool } -> List.List (Html.Attribute (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)) -> List.List (Html.Html Basics.Never) -> Html.Html (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)

values.name: options
values.comment:
A wrapper for the list of options in the menu.
This should be a direct parent of your list of option nodes.
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Token option selection -> List.List (Html.Attribute (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)) -> List.List (Html.Html (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)) -> Html.Html (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)

values.name: optionsDiv
values.comment:
A wrapper for the list of options in the menu.
This should be a direct parent of your list of option nodes.
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Token option selection -> List.List (Html.Attribute (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)) -> List.List (Html.Html (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)) -> Html.Html (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)

values.name: token
values.comment: Create a token manually for use in view and helper functions
values.type: { model : Preset.Combobox.Model option, config : Preset.Combobox.Context -> Preset.Combobox.Config option selection, options : List.List option, selected : selection } -> Preset.Combobox.Token option selection

values.name: update
values.comment:
Call this in your own update function, it returns the latest selection, 
    the dropdown model, and `Cmd`s to pass on. You will need to save the selection and menu in your model.
values.type: Preset.Combobox.Msg option -> { model : Preset.Combobox.Model option, config : Preset.Combobox.Context -> Preset.Combobox.Config option selection, options : List.List option, selected : selection } -> ( selection, Preset.Combobox.Model option, Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Preset.Combobox.Msg option) )

values.name: view
values.comment: This needs to wrap your menu. It does not render any HTML, but provides a [#Token](#Token) that you pass into view functions, and also the currently visible options.
values.type: { model : Preset.Combobox.Model option, config : Preset.Combobox.Context -> Preset.Combobox.Config option selection, options : List.List option, selected : selection } -> (Preset.Combobox.Token option selection -> List.List option -> Html.Html (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)) -> Html.Html (Preset.Combobox.Msg option)

name: Preset.Listbox

unions.name: Config
unions.comment:
Created in [#config](#config)  
    This is passed into [#update](#update) and [#view](#view) and is used to control how the menu reacts to user input

unions.name: Model
unions.comment:
Stores whether the menu is open or not, and which option is currently focussed.  
    It does _not_ store which option is selected.  
    Is updated and returned in the [#update](#update) function.

unions.name: Token
unions.comment: Created automatically when you call [#view](#view), pass it in to view functions

aliases.name: OptionConfig
aliases.comment:
Passed in to [#config](#config)  
    The `id` field must be unique, and is displayed in the HTML, used for accessibility  
    The `optionToLabel` field allows users to use keyboard input to search for options

values.name: button
values.comment: The button used to open and close the menu
values.type: Preset.Listbox.Token option -> List.List (Html.Attribute Preset.Listbox.Msg) -> List.List (Html.Html Preset.Listbox.Msg) -> Html.Html Preset.Listbox.Msg

values.name: config
values.comment: Create a [#Config](#Config) type
values.type: Preset.Listbox.OptionConfig option -> Preset.Listbox.Config option

values.name: focussedOption
values.comment: Which option is focussed (returns an index)
values.type: Preset.Listbox.Token option -> Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int

values.name: init
values.comment: Initialise a closed menu, you can use this in your own init function
values.type: Preset.Listbox.Model

values.name: isOpen
values.comment: Check whether the menu is open or closed
values.type: Preset.Listbox.Token option -> Basics.Bool

values.name: option
values.comment: A focusable and selectable option
values.type: Preset.Listbox.Token option -> { value : Basics.Int, isSelected : Basics.Bool } -> List.List (Html.Attribute Preset.Listbox.Msg) -> List.List (Html.Html Basics.Never) -> Html.Html Preset.Listbox.Msg

values.name: options
values.comment:
A wrapper for the list of options in the menu.
This should be a direct parent of your list of option nodes.
values.type: Preset.Listbox.Token option -> List.List (Html.Attribute Preset.Listbox.Msg) -> List.List (Html.Html Preset.Listbox.Msg) -> Html.Html Preset.Listbox.Msg

values.name: update
values.comment:
Call this in your own update function, it returns the list of options with the correct selection, 
    the dropdown model, and `Cmd`s to pass on. You will need to save the updated options and menu in your model.
values.type: Preset.Listbox.Msg -> { model : Preset.Listbox.Model, config : Preset.Listbox.Config option, options : ZipList.ZipList option } -> ( ZipList.ZipList option, Preset.Listbox.Model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd Preset.Listbox.Msg )

values.name: view
values.comment: This needs to wrap your menu. It does not render any HTML, but provides a [#Token](#Token) that you pass into view functions, and also the current options.
values.type: { model : Preset.Listbox.Model, config : Preset.Listbox.Config option, options : ZipList.ZipList option } -> (Preset.Listbox.Token option -> ZipList.ZipList option -> Html.Html Preset.Listbox.Msg) -> Html.Html Preset.Listbox.Msg

name: Preset.Menu
comment:
Menus!

@docs Model, Msg, Config, init, update, config


# Views

The view is highly customisable. You decide how to structure the menu & how it is styled.
There a few caveats here to note:

  - Displaying the menu always open, without [#button](#button) to manage state, is unsupported
    It is possible with a rework, so depends on demand. If this is something you would really want, please open an issue.
  - All of [#button](#button), [#options](#options) and [#link](#link) must be present
  - [#options](#options) is a `ul` node, it must take a list of only [#link](#link) and other `li` nodes to be valid HTML

```
Preset.Menu.view model
    menuConfig
    (\token ->
        Html.div
            [ Attr.class "..."
            ]
            [ Preset.Menu.button token
                [ Attr.class "..."
                ]
                [ Html.text "Examples"
                ]
            , Html.button
                [ Attr.class "..."
                , Key.tabbable False
                ]
                [ if Preset.Menu.isOpen token then
                    Examples.Svg.chevronUp

                  else
                    Examples.Svg.chevronDown
                ]
            , Preset.Menu.options token
                [ Attr.class "..."
                , Attr.classList
                    [ ( "...", Preset.Menu.isOpen token )
                    , ( "...", not (Preset.Menu.isOpen token) )
                    ]
                ]
                (List.indexedMap
                    (\idx option ->
                        Preset.Menu.link token
                            { idx = idx }
                            []
                            (\link ->
                                link
                                    [ Attr.href "/"
                                    , Attr.class "..."
                                    ]
                                    [ Html.text option.label ]
                            )
                    )
                    Examples.MenuItem.list
                )
            ]
    )
```

@docs Token, isOpen, focussedOption, view, button, options, link

unions.name: Model
unions.comment:
Holds information about whether the menu is open or closed.
Should be kept in your Model.

values.name: button
values.type: Preset.Menu.Token option -> List.List (Html.Attribute Preset.Menu.Msg) -> List.List (Html.Html Preset.Menu.Msg) -> Html.Html Preset.Menu.Msg

values.name: config
values.comment:
Pass in an id to uniquely identity this dropdown.
It _must_ be unique to the page or weird things will happen.
values.type: String.String -> Preset.Menu.Config option

values.name: focussedOption
values.comment:
Returns the currently focussed option. This provides consistency with other dropdowns
like [Preset.Combobox](Preset-Combobox) or [Preset.Listbox](Preset-Listbox), but menu items
are different from the other dropdowns in that they are focussed directly, so for styling you just need
to use a CSS focus selector.

    link
        [ Attr.href "/"
        , Attr.class "bg-transparent focus:bg-purple-100"
        ]
        [ Html.text option.label ]
values.type: Preset.Menu.Token option -> Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int

values.name: init
values.comment: Initialise a closed menu
values.type: Preset.Menu.Model

values.name: isOpen
values.comment:
Returns whether the menu state is open or closed
You can use this in your view to only show the options list when the menu state is open, or switch chevron direction

    Html.button
        [ Attr.class "..."
        , Key.tabbable False
        ]
        [ if Preset.Menu.isOpen token then
            Examples.Svg.chevronUp

          else
            Examples.Svg.chevronDown
        ]
values.type: Preset.Menu.Token option -> Basics.Bool

values.name: link
values.comment:
Currently menus only support link children
Takes the token returned from [#view](#view), the current option index, a list of HTML attributes, and a function.
The function argument is in effect an Html.a node and can be used like a normal HTML node, although cannot be nested.

    Preset.Menu.link token
        { idx = idx }
        []
        (\link ->
            link
                [ Attr.href "/"
                , Attr.class "bg-transparent focus:bg-purple-100"
                ]
                [ Html.text option.label ]
        )
values.type: Preset.Menu.Token options -> { idx : Basics.Int } -> List.List (Html.Attribute Preset.Menu.Msg) -> ((List.List (Html.Attribute Preset.Menu.Msg) -> List.List (Html.Html Basics.Never) -> Menus.Menu.Link Preset.Menu.Msg) -> Menus.Menu.Link Preset.Menu.Msg) -> Html.Html Preset.Menu.Msg

values.name: options
values.comment:
A wrapper for the list of options in the menu.
This should be a direct parent of your list of option nodes.

    Preset.Menu.options token
        [ Attr.class "..."
        , Attr.classList
            [ ( "...", Preset.Menu.isOpen token )
            , ( "...", not (Preset.Menu.isOpen token) )
            ]
        ]
        (List.indexedMap
            (\idx option ->
                Preset.Menu.link token
                    { idx = idx }
                    []
                    (\link ->
                        link
                            [ Attr.href "/"
                            , Attr.class "bg-transparent focus:bg-purple-100"
                            ]
                            [ Html.text option.label ]
                    )
            )
            Examples.MenuItem.list
        )
values.type: Preset.Menu.Token option -> List.List (Html.Attribute Preset.Menu.Msg) -> List.List (Html.Html Preset.Menu.Msg) -> Html.Html Preset.Menu.Msg

values.name: update
values.comment:
Handles updating menu and focus state.
Call this in your update function.
values.type: Preset.Menu.Msg -> { model : Preset.Menu.Model, config : Preset.Menu.Config option, options : List.List option } -> ( Preset.Menu.Model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd Preset.Menu.Msg )

values.name: view
values.comment:
This takes the menu model & config and gives you a token
you can pass into the functions that make up a complete menu.
Use [#button](#button), [#options](#options) and [#link](#link) to form a complete menu

    Preset.Menu.view model menuConfig
        (\token ->
            ... view code here
        )
values.type: { model : Preset.Menu.Model, config : Preset.Menu.Config option } -> (Preset.Menu.Token option -> Html.Html Preset.Menu.Msg) -> Html.Html Preset.Menu.Msg
